sourceMovie.title: Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Warlock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters clicked for you, Warlock (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters clicked for you, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters?
faq.answer: Try Warlock, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest, The Witch—each shares DNA with Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters?
faq.answer: Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters (2013) is best described as Fantasy, Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hansel & Gretel: Witch Hunters worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
